claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you have and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 18 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask some follow-up questions as eligibility rules differ for children over 16.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This would include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- T-levels
- Scottish Highers
- Home education (if it started before they turned 16)
- Certain training courses with an approved provider

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He actually works full-time now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me make my assessment:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result

I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

Child Benefit can be claimed:
- Until a child turns 16, OR
- Until they turn 20 if they continue in approved education or training

Since Alex is 18 years old and is working full-time rather than being in approved education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria. Child Benefit entitlement would have ended when he either turned 16 or when he left approved education/training (whichever came later).

If Alex's circumstances change in the future and he returns to approved education or training before turning 20, you may be able to claim Child Benefit again at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit until a child turns 16, or until they turn 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 16 (he is 18) and is not in approved education or training - he is working full-time instead - he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and working full-time. He is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility ends at age 16 unless the child continues in approved education or training (up to age 20). Since Alex does not meet these criteria, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit until a child turns 16, or until they turn 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 16 (he is 18) and is not in approved education or training - he is working full-time instead - he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and working full-time. He is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility ends at age 16 unless the child continues in approved education or training (up to age 20). Since Alex does not meet these criteria, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
